mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an operator for managing PostgreSQL databases on Kubernetes, providing a seamless experience for deployment and maintenance. It automates database operations through direct integration with the Kubernetes API, making it suitable for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the reliance on Kubernetes-native patterns for database management.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the repository containing the CloudNativePG charts.
> This command updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator into the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cloudnative-pg

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the CloudNativePG operator.
> Installs the CloudNativePG operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: "14"
EOF

```

> This command creates a PostgreSQL cluster named 'my-postgres-cluster' with 3 instances.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: "14"
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

kubectl describe cluster my-postgres-cluster -n cloudnative-pg

```

> This command lists the PostgreSQL clusters in the specified namespace.
> This command provides detailed information about the created cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL cluster and all data associated with it.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> Uninstalls the CloudNativePG operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your PostgreSQL data. This step includes the upgrade process and verification.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backs up the current cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the CloudNativePG operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the PostgreSQL cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster creation failed due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Kubernetes nodes have enough resources (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'PostgreSQL cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The PostgreSQL instances may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the PostgreSQL pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cloudnative-pg'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Helm chart using the install command provided in the installation steps.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified PostgreSQL cluster may not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster name and namespace using 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for PostgreSQL 15, improved backup and restore functionality.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the PostgreSQL cluster listed with 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg', showing its status as 'Ready'.

apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: "14"
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
